pr.title: Match some very easy parts of itzrshell
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Matched a small set of straightforward itzrshell item functions by implementing them as thin wrappers around existing itzgshell item routines, one stage/generator routine, and a generic item reference helper. The PR also added headers/prototypes for itzrshell, itzgshell, and grZakoGenerator dependencies so these cross-file calls could compile cleanly.
what_changed: Added implementations for itzrshell functions it_802E02E8 through it_802E0468 in src/melee/it/items/itzrshell.c.

Most new itzrshell functions simply forward their Item_GObj* argument to matching itzgshell functions such as it_802DE040, it_802DE434, it_802DE4FC, it_802DE648, it_802DEEF4, it_802DFC34, it_802DFC58, it_802DFC90, it_802DFCB0, it_802DFD60, and it_802DFE40.
Added it_802E0388 as a wrapper around grZakoGenerator_801CACB8, casting the Item_GObj* argument to Ground_GObj*.
Added it_802E0468 as a wrapper around it_8026B894(gobj, ref_gobj).
Created itzrshell.h with include guards, platform/types includes, and prototypes for the newly matched itzrshell functions.
Created itzgshell.h with include guards and prototypes for the existing itzgshell functions used by itzrshell.c.
Changed itzgshell.c from effectively empty to include its own header.
Added gr/types.h include and a prototype for grZakoGenerator_801CACB8(Ground_GObj* gobj) in grzakogenerator.h.
smart_moves: Recognized that the target itzrshell functions were not unique logic but simple aliases/wrappers over already-known green shell item functions, enabling quick exact matches with minimal code.
Pulled required callee declarations into headers instead of relying on implicit declarations, which keeps matching work type-safe and easier to reuse.
Used a direct cast to Ground_GObj* only at the one cross-system call site, making the unusual item-to-ground dependency visible and localized.
Kept the implementation minimal: each function forwards arguments directly with no unnecessary temporaries or control flow, which is often the best tactic for matching trivial wrapper functions.
decomp_lessons: When a related item variant has nearly identical behavior, check whether its functions are thin wrappers around the already-decompiled sibling item before attempting a full reconstruction.
Wrapper functions can often be matched by preserving exact call shape: same argument order, no extra locals, and direct return/void forwarding.
Cross-module wrappers may require adding prototypes in the callee module's header first; matching item code can expose missing stage/ground declarations.
Header work is part of matching work: adding the right prototypes in itzgshell.h and grzakogenerator.h made itzrshell.c express the dependency graph clearly.
Evidence shows no PR discussion or review comments, so there is no documented reviewer rationale beyond the diff itself.
naming_conventions: Unidentified functions retained address-based names using the project convention, e.g. it_802E02E8, it_802DE040, grZakoGenerator_801CACB8.
Item object parameters are named gobj and typed as Item_GObj*.
Reference object parameters use ref_gobj and HSD_GObj*.
Ground object parameters use Ground_GObj* in the stage header.
New item headers use lower-case include guards matching the filename style shown in the diff, e.g. _itzrshell_h_ and _itzgshell_h_.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Implemented address-named functions as single-call void wrappers to reproduce trivial branch/call behavior.
Reused already-existing itzgshell routines for itzrshell behavior instead of duplicating logic.
Used an explicit cast from Item_GObj* to Ground_GObj* for grZakoGenerator_801CACB8, likely to match the known function signature while preserving the wrapper's item-facing signature.
Included only necessary headers in itzrshell.c: its own header, sibling shell header, grZakoGenerator header, and it_26B1 helper header.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching itzrshell.h to see whether the missing prototype for it_802E0448 was added or intentionally omitted.
Search for other shell item PRs that use itzgshell as a base implementation for variant-specific wrapper matching.
Inspect grZakoGenerator_801CACB8 usage sites to understand why an itzrshell wrapper casts Item_GObj* to Ground_GObj*.
Look for subsequent renames of it_802E02E8 through it_802E0468 once shell item behavior was better understood.
